LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS TERILYN JOE & LINDA LUOMA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF S. CLINTON WOODS SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1324. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS SIMPLY AMPLIFY THE ALLEGATIONS ABOUT THE "RELATED BUILDING SYSTEMS." PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT CHANGING THE TENOR OF THE CASE OR UNDERCUTTING THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT GRANTS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD THE MISAPPROPRIATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION TO AMEND IS NOT THE VEHICLE TO RESOLVE THE UNDERLYING MERITS OF THAT CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. THE NOTICE OF MOTION INDICATES THAT DEFENDANT IS SEEKING TO ADJUDICATE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF BUT IT DOES NOT ADDRESS PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS REGARDING "RELATED BUILDING SYSTEMS." (COMPLAINT, 17:4-5). IN OTHER WORDS, THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. EVEN IF THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE MOTION ALSO SEEKS TO ADJUDICATE AN ISSUE OF DUTY REGARDING THE WINDOWS, THE MOTION FAILS. DEFENDANT IS CORRECT THAT IT WOULD NOT BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE WINDOWS IF THEY WERE DEFECTIVE. UNDER CC 1364 AND DOVER VILLAGE ASSN. V. JENNISON (2010) 191 CAL.APP.4TH 123, 126, THE OBLIGATION TO REPAIR A DEFECTIVE WINDOW WOULD FALL UPON PLAINTIFFS. THE COURT REJECTS PLAINTIFFS' ARGUMENT THAT THE CC&RS MODIFIED THE STATUTORY SCHEME. AT RFA 11, PLAINTIFFS ADMIT THAT THE CC&RS DO NOT MODIFY THE STATUTORY DUTIES AND THAT ADMISSION IS CONCLUSIVE. SEE CCP 2033.410(A). NEVERTHELESS, DEFENDANTS MAY STILL HAVE A DUTY TO REPAIR AND REPLACE THE WINDOWS IF ITS TORTIOUS CONDUCT IN MANAGING THE GENERAL COMMON AREAS (AS OPPOSED TO THE EXCLUSIVE USE COMMON AREAS) CAUSED THE DAMAGE. THE RECORD IS DEVOID OF EVIDENCE TO RESOLVE THAT FACTUAL ISSUE REGARDING CAUSATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. S. CLINTON WOODS OF THE BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 433-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. THEODORE A. KOLB & ATTY. JOSH MALTZER OF THE HANSON BRIDGETT LLP, TEL. NO. 995-5098 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants' Motion For Summary Adjudication is denied because defendant failed to shift the burden. The notice of motion indicates that defendant is seeking to adjudicate the cause of action for declaratory relief but it does not address Plaintiffs' claims regarding "related building systems." (Complaint, 17:4-5). In other words, the motion does not completely dispose of the cause of action. Even if the Court concludes that the motion also seeks to adjudicate an issue of duty regarding the windows, the motion fails. Defendant is correct that it would not be responsible for the windows if they were defective. Under CC 1364 and Dover Village Assn. v. Jennison (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 123, 126, the obligation to repair a defective window would fall upon Plaintiffs. The Court rejects plaintiffs' argument that the CC&Rs modified the statutory scheme. At RFA 11, Plaintiffs admit that the CC&Rs do not modify the statutory duties and that admission is conclusive. See CCP 2033.410(a). Nevertheless, defendants may still have a duty to repair and replace the windows if its tortious conduct in managing the general common areas (as opposed to the exclusive use common areas) caused the damage. The record is devoid of evidence to resolve that factual issue regarding causation. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
=(302/EHG)
